descript.abstract: This point feature class contains the locations of all 87 experimental forests, ranges and watersheds, including cooperating experimental areas.
descript.purpose: These data were prepared to show locations for all experimental forests, ranges, and watersheds managed by the US Forest Service (USFS) or in a cooperating agreement with the USFS. These data also provide spatial and descriptive attributes for each experimental area. The purpose of the data is to provide the location and descriptions of these experimental areas for Forest Service employees and the public.

idinfo.native: Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 Version 6.1 (Build 7601) Service Pack 1; Esri ArcGIS 10.3.1.4959

attracc.attraccr: As far as is known, experimental area locations and attributes are current as of 2017.

dataqual.complete: As far as is known, experimental area locations and attributes are complete as of 2017.

procstep.procdesc: Used a list of locations and geographic locations (latitudes and longitudes) to create a geodatabase. Added the Denbigh Experimental Forest. Also added contact information for each area.

spdoinfo.indspref: Points representing the locations of experimental forests, ranges, and watersheds.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

overview.eaover: This geodatabase contains the locations of 87 experimental areas including forests, ranges, and watersheds. It includes all associated cooperating experimental areas (areas that are not owned or managed by the US Forest Service).

Attribute Defintions: “Name” = Name of experimental area “Type” = Experimental area type. Values are: Experimental Forest Experimental Range Experimental Watershed Cooperating Experimental Area “State” = US state or territory in which the experimental area is located “Station” = US Forest Service research organization the experimental area is administratively part of; values are: NRS = Northern Research Station SRS = Southern Research Station RMRS = Rocky Mountain Research Station PNW = Pacific Northwest Research Station PSW = Pacific Southwest Research Station IITF = International Institute of Forestry “Established” = Year of official establishment [YYYY] “Land_Ownership” = Owner/manager of the experimental area “Lead_Scientist” = Name of lead scientist “Hectares” = Area of experimental area in hectares “Acres” = Area of experimental area in acres “Region” = US Forest Service administrative region; if the area is not part of the USFS, the value is "N/A" “Contact_Info” = Contact information for the experimental area; this usually refers to the lead scientist “Lat” = Latitude [Decimal Degrees] “Long” = Longitude [Decimal Degrees]

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
